subject: Human Resources
title: From Personnel to Human Resources
description: A micro-lesson that makes From Personnel to Human Resources usable.

hook.text: In the 1980s, companies started renaming 'personnel departments' to 'human resources.' It wasn't just cosmetic.

buildup.text: The shift reflected a new philosophy: employees are resources to develop, not just records to maintain.

discovery.text: HR took on strategic roles—workforce planning, culture shaping, and organizational development.

twist.text: Critics argued 'human resources' was dehumanizing—treating people as inputs alongside capital and equipment.

climax.text: Despite the debate, the rebranding marked HR's evolution from back-office function to business partner.

punchline.text: The name changed because the job changed.

quiz.question: Why were personnel departments renamed to human resources?
options: To reflect a shift from administration to strategic people management
It was just a marketing trend
